summary: Radix Colors ships 31 twelve-step scales (6 tinted grays: gray, mauve, slate, sage, olive, sand; 25 chromatic hues), each in light and dark versions with solid, alpha, P3 and P3-alpha variants, plus blackA/whiteA overlay scales. Every step has a documented role: 1-2 app backgrounds, 3-5 component backgrounds (normal/hover/pressed), 6-8 borders (8 for stronger borders and focus rings), 9-10 solid backgrounds (9 is the highest-chroma step), 11-12 text.

evidence.excerpt: Steps 1-2 are designed for app backgrounds and subtle component backgrounds; step 9 has the highest chroma of all steps in the scale; step 8 for stronger borders on interactive components and focus rings.
evidence.note: Per-step role table for all 12 steps.

evidence.excerpt: export const blue = { blue1: "#fbfdff", blue2: "#f4faff", ... }; export const blueA = { blueA1: "#0080ff04", ... }; export const blueP3 = { blue1: "color(display-p3 0.986 0.992 0.999)", ... }
evidence.note: 31 scale families exported, each as solid/A/P3/P3A objects of 12 steps.

summary: The published scales are static hand-authored tables — the repo ships literal hex/P3 values with no generation code, and no authoring methodology is documented. Releases describe wholesale manual reworks ('a complete rework of all colors' in both 2.0.0 and 3.0.0). A hosted custom-palette tool generates full 12-step scales from 'a couple reference colors', but its algorithm is closed; the docs say only that generated scales 'are based on the Radix Colors scales themselves'.

evidence.excerpt: Use the custom color palette tool to generate a Radix Colors scale based just on a couple reference colors. [...] The generated scales are based on the Radix Colors scales themselves.
evidence.note: Tool is hosted at radix-ui.com/colors/custom; no algorithm published.

evidence.excerpt: 2.0.0 (Aug 2023): A complete rework of all colors, dramatically improving contrast across the board. 3.0.0 (Oct 2023): A complete rework of all colors.
evidence.note: Rework language implies manual curation rather than parameter changes to an open algorithm.
Repo contains only literal value tables (light.ts, dark.ts, blackA.ts, whiteA.ts, index.ts) — no generation code.

notes: 'Hand-authored' is inferred from the absence of generation code and the rework-style changelog; the team has never published its authoring process.

summary: Shipped formats are sRGB hex (solid), 8-digit hex (alpha), and color(display-p3 ...) for the wide-gamut variants added in 3.0.0 ('Add P3 colorspace versions of all scales' — every scale has P3 and P3-alpha versions). The authoring/working color space is not published. Radix Themes gates P3 values behind @supports (color: color(display-p3 1 1 1)) plus @media (color-gamut: p3).

evidence.excerpt: 3.0.0 (October 2, 2023): Add P3 colorspace versions of all scales.

evidence.note: P3 delivery mechanism; even 'transparent' is redefined in P3 to avoid Chrome box-shadow transition bugs.

guaranteedPairings: step 11 text on step 2 background: APCA Lc 60
step 12 text on step 2 background: APCA Lc 90
step 9 solid backgrounds pair with white foreground (except sky, mint, lime, yellow, amber, which pair with dark text)

summary: Text contrast is guaranteed by construction against APCA, not WCAG 2.x: the docs state that steps 11 and 12 'are guaranteed to Lc 60 and Lc 90 APCA contrast ratio on top of a step 2 background from the same scale'. Step-9 solid backgrounds have designated foreground colors (white for most scales, dark text for five bright hues), which Radix Themes operationalizes as the --accent-contrast token. No tooling-based checking is documented — the guarantee is baked into the hand-tuned values.

evidence.excerpt: Steps 11 and 12—which are designed for text—are guaranteed to Lc 60 and Lc 90 APCA contrast ratio on top of a step 2 background from the same scale. [...] Sky, Mint, Lime, Yellow, and Amber are designed for dark foreground text.
2.0.0: A complete rework of all colors, dramatically improving contrast across the board.
evidence.note: The APCA targets date from the 2.0 rework era.

summary: Three effective layers, two of them semantic: primitive scales from @radix-ui/colors; role scales where the chosen accent and gray are remapped step-for-step onto --accent-1..12 / --gray-1..12 (plus the parallel --focus-1..12 scale) via [data-accent-color] selectors; and functional tokens for backgrounds, surfaces, panels, overlays, and accent extras (--accent-contrast, --accent-surface, --accent-indicator, --accent-track). There is no component color-token layer — components consume role and functional tokens directly.

evidence.excerpt: [data-accent-color='blue'] { --accent-1: var(--blue-1); ... } / [data-accent-color]:where(:not(...)) { --focus-1: var(--accent-1); ... --focus-8: var(--accent-8); } / :where(.radix-themes) { --color-background: white; --color-panel-solid: white; --color-surface: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.85); }

evidence.note: Docs also describe 'color aliasing' — remapping Radix tokens onto preferred names.

summary: Radix Themes tokenizes color, space (--space-1..9), radius (--radius-1..6 plus --radius-factor/--radius-full/--radius-thumb), shadows (--shadow-1..6), typography (font families, sizes, weights, letter-spacing, code-specific vars), cursors (unusually, one token per interactive component kind), and a global --scaling factor. Motion, breakpoints, z-index and opacity are not tokenized — breakpoints exist only as responsive prop syntax, not CSS variables.

evidence.excerpt: tokens/: base.css, color.css, colors/, cursor.css, radius.css, scaling.css, shadow.css, space.css, typography.css
evidence.note: Per-file variable inventories confirmed: --space-1..9, --radius-1..6 + factor/full/thumb, --shadow-1..6, --cursor-button etc., --scaling. No motion or breakpoint token file exists.

answer.pattern: --{scale}-{step} and --{scale}-a{step} for primitives; --accent-{step} / --gray-{step} / --focus-{step} for role scales; --color-{purpose} and --accent-{purpose} for functional tokens

summary: Kebab-case CSS custom properties throughout (the 1.0.0 release standardized gray1 → gray-1). Alpha variants use an 'a' infix on the step. Theming is fully runtime: light/dark via .light/.dark (or .light-theme/.dark-theme) class switching, accent/gray/radius choices via data attributes ([data-accent-color], [data-radius]) set by the Theme component, all resolving through CSS custom properties.

evidence.excerpt: 1.0.0 (June 23, 2023): CSS variable names changed from gray1 to gray-1; file names from grayDarkA.css to gray-dark-alpha.css.

summary: Light and dark ship as separately hand-tuned palettes (dark.ts values are independent, not transforms of light.ts), switched by class; semantic tokens like --color-background re-point on top of the swapped scales. There is no dedicated high-contrast mode — instead components accept a highContrast prop. OS sync is explicitly delegated: the docs call system-appearance matching 'a deceptively complex problem' and recommend next-themes class switching, warning 'Do not try to set <Theme appearance={resolvedTheme}>'.

evidence.excerpt: By default, the root Theme appearance is light. [...] a deceptively complex problem to solve given SSR, SSG and client side hydration considerations. [...] Do not try to set <Theme appearance={resolvedTheme}>. Instead, rely just on class switching that next-themes provides.

evidence.note: light.ts and dark.ts are parallel hand-authored tables; dark values are not derived from light values by any code in the repo.

answer.colorSource: --focus-8 (a full --focus-1..12 scale remapped from the accent scale)

summary: Focus rings are outlines gated on :focus-visible: base-button.css uses 'outline: 2px solid var(--focus-8); outline-offset: 2px'. The color comes from a dedicated focus scale — --focus-1..12 is remapped step-for-step from the accent scale in color.css — matching the scale documentation that assigns step 8 to 'stronger borders and focus rings'. Touch devices get a separate affordance: an 0.5em outline of --accent-a4 on :active for coarse pointers.

evidence.excerpt: &:where(:focus-visible) { outline: 2px solid var(--focus-8); outline-offset: 2px; } / @media (pointer: coarse) { &:where(:active:not([data-state='open'])) { outline: 0.5em solid var(--accent-a4); outline-offset: 0; } }

summary: Button touches only the semantic layers — role scales and functional tokens, never raw scale names: solid is background --accent-9 / color --accent-contrast, hover --accent-10, highContrast swaps to --accent-12 with --gray-1 text; disabled uses --gray-a8 on --gray-2; classic builds its depth from --gray-a* inset shadows. Seven style variants times 26 accentColor options (25 hues + gray) times an optional highContrast boolean. No hardcoded color literals beyond 'transparent' in gradients.

evidence.excerpt: background-color: var(--accent-9); color: var(--accent-contrast); [...] &:where(:hover) { background-color: var(--accent-10); } [...] color: var(--gray-a8); background-color: var(--gray-2);
